text:
In a forgotten town, nestled between rolling hills and ancient forests, lived a man named Harold Humphrey. Harold was not exactly celebrated for his accomplishments—mostly forgotten after losing a bet to Edgar Wigglesworth on a bet about their favorite baseball team. But Harold harbored a passion nobody understood—a passion for collecting forgotten legends, forgotten songs, and, oddly enough, antique gargoyles.

One afternoon, while sifting through an old chest labeled "Hallucinations Historical," Harold discovered something extraordinary—a rare vinyl record emitting melodies that whispered of ancient lore. Intrigued, he hurried home, armed with headphones.

That evening, he put on the record. Stars swirled overhead as it played. Then figures, vibrant yet ancient, emerged from the grooves. They danced, laughed, sang a haunting melody that made Harold forget his worry about money. Hours seemed to dissolve.

The next morning, inspired by their mysterious escapades, Harold put together an album. He titled it *"From
